query: I'm rolling off the incident-rank-validation project at the end of the month. There's no specific successor — whoever picks this up will probably be a new hire about 6 months from now. The project is a NUTS-sampler-based scoring pipeline that's been running for 8 months. Help me write the handoff doc.
expected_behavior: Produces a primer with the required sections: a 3-sentence purpose, 5-8 founding principles (each with WHY plus a rejected trade-off, not just a feature list), and 3-5 where-to-start entry points naming concrete file paths or run commands

Includes an explicit defense-in-depth false-confidence section that names at least one layer of redundancy AND the single brittle component the redundancy could mask (NOT just a list of safety layers as strengths)

Includes a glossary of project-specific terms AND a 'what is NOT here' list that captures descoped areas, rejected alternatives, or intentional gaps — so the successor does not re-open closed decisions
